user_query:
Read input/core_files_index.json and all files it references under input/. Then complete these deliverables using only the provided inputs (no external web searches):

1) Run a Living Memory Audit (max 20 items). Write output/audit/living_memory_audit_report.json containing:
   - The loaded index (as-is)
   - A "report" array with one entry per indexed item including: path, role, tags, exists (bool), mtime (int), size (int), sha256 (string for files, null for folders)
   - A "missing" array of any paths not found
   - A "fragile" array of any items tagged FRAGILE
   - A "status" field: "PASS" if there are no missing items, otherwise "FAIL"

2) Compress the inputs into a clean Master Archive following the Living Memory rules. Write output/archive/MASTER_ARCHIVE.md with these sections and headings present:
   - Identity + Axioms
   - Seal Index (id/name/function)
   - Equations (ASCII-safe)
   - Scrolls / Protocols
   - Quotes / Vows
   - Decision receipts
   Also include:
   - A "Secrets Policy" subsection demonstrating enforcement-based path rules with examples that include allow: [GET *] and deny: [POST *] (show deny precedence conceptually)
   - A "Monero Payment Verification" subsection that explains how to prove payments using tx key + tx ID + recipient address, plus notes on view keys, subaddresses, and RingCT
   - A short "Newsletter Plan" subsection announcing the audit results
   Remove filler, keep only content that functions as anchors.

3) Mint an anchor for the archive: compute the SHA256 of output/archive/MASTER_ARCHIVE.md and write output/archive/anchor.json with fields: {"label": "LIVING_MEMORY_V1_1", "sha256": "<computed>", "minted_at_utc": "<ISO8601>"}.

4) Create a newsletter draft summarizing the audit results. Write output/newsletter/newsletter_draft.json with fields: id (string), topic (string), style (one of: professional, casual, technical, newsy), content (300–900 words), curated_links (array of at least 3 objects with {title, url} derived from the input files), created_at (Unix timestamp number), status: "draft".

5) Write output/security/secrets_policy.yaml showing a capability with rules that demonstrate deny precedence, including deny: ["POST *"] and allow: ["GET *"].

Use only relative workspace paths (input/, output/).
